title: Lasiosiphon anthylloides
taxonomy: Lasiosiphon anthylloides is a plant species within the family Convolvulaceae. It belongs to the genus Lasiosiphon, which is a group of flowering plants. The Convolvulaceae family is a large group of flowering plants that also includes morning glories, sweet potatoes, and bindweeds. Lasiosiphon anthylloides is native to tropical Africa and is a climbing or scrambling plant. The classification of Lasiosiphon anthylloides is still being researched and studied by botanists.

intro: Lasiosiphon anthylloides is a plant species commonly known as the "Devil's Claw" or "Wood Spurge," belonging to the family Apocynaceae. Traditionally, Lasiosiphon anthylloides has been used to alleviate various health issues, including pain, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include harpagoside, a unique iridoid glycoside with analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
